# Day 2 transcript fixtures

Ten short witness-style transcripts (50 to 300 words) used for evaluating the
structured-extraction pass against `spec/incident-schema.json`.

## Provenance

All ten transcripts in this directory are **synthesized** from publicly
documented incident-report narrative patterns. None reproduce a specific
identifiable real-world report. Names, addresses, dates, vehicle identifiers,
and other identifying details are fictional. Locations are generic
city/street patterns. The synthesis is grounded in the structure and
vocabulary of public-domain incident-reporting corpora listed below so the
language matches what a real witness intake would produce.

The build guide (Day 2) explicitly permits synthesis when public sourcing is
not feasible: "If you cannot source 10 real ones, source as many as you can
and synthesize the remainder from real OSHA narrative text with light
rephrasing; mark synthesized ones in the README."

## Pattern sources

| Transcript | Pattern source | Public URL |
|---|---|---|
| 01-fall-from-elevation.txt | OSHA fatality and catastrophe investigation summaries (fall hazards) | https://www.osha.gov/ords/imis/accidentsearch.html |
| 02-chemical-release.txt | EPA enforcement case narratives (Clean Air Act, unpermitted release) | https://www.epa.gov/enforcement/cases-and-settlements |
| 03-wage-theft.txt | DOL Wage and Hour Division complaint intake guidance | https://www.dol.gov/agencies/whd/contact/complaints |
| 04-harassment.txt | EEOC charge of discrimination intake narrative | https://www.eeoc.gov/filing-charge-discrimination |
| 05-property-damage.txt | Municipal incident-report templates (vehicle into fixed object) | (general municipal pattern) |
| 06-machine-guarding.txt | OSHA 29 CFR 1910.212 citation narratives | https://www.osha.gov/laws-regs/regulations/standardnumber/1910/1910.212 |
| 07-illegal-discharge.txt | EPA Clean Water Act §402 enforcement narratives | https://www.epa.gov/npdes |
| 08-vehicle-defect.txt | NHTSA vehicle owner questionnaire (VOQ) submissions | https://www.nhtsa.gov/recalls |
| 09-gas-leak.txt | PHMSA distribution-pipeline incident reports | https://www.phmsa.dot.gov/data-and-statistics |
| 10-playground-hazard.txt | CPSC public playground safety handbook hazard categories | https://www.cpsc.gov/Safety-Education/Safety-Education-Centers/Playground-Safety |

## File layout

- `NN-<slug>.txt`: one transcript each, plain UTF-8.
- `expected.json`: hand-labeled expected `incident_type` per transcript.
- `results.md`: evaluation harness output, regenerated by
  `cargo run -p witness-eval -- --transcripts-dir tests/fixtures/transcripts --schema spec/incident-schema.json --output tests/fixtures/transcripts/results.md`.

## Note on `incident_type` mapping

The schema enum is intentionally coarse. Vehicle defects (transcript 08) do
not have a dedicated category and are mapped to `other`. The model is allowed
to choose `safety_hazard` for transcript 08 with no penalty in the confusion
matrix beyond the standard misclassification count.
